title: Card Shopper - curated trading card marketplace (web + app)

client.industry: Trading card collectibles marketplace (sports, Pokemon, MTG)
client.signals: Non-technical founder with a clear product vision and a named brand (Card Shopper). Has done market thinking: a fee-savings pitch doc already exists and a founding-seller outreach plan (10 to 20 sellers) is in place. Values trust/curation (manual seller verification, not an open marketplace). Budget-conscious and pragmatic: explicitly wants phased delivery so a usable core ships within budget. Wants the online experience to feel like an in-person card show.

summary: A curated online marketplace that recreates the feel of an in-person card show for collectors of sports cards, Pokemon, and Magic: The Gathering. Buyers browse verified shops on a US map, then dig through inventory via a face-down card flip experience or a grid, and can watchlist, message, offer, or buy instantly. Sellers are manually verified, get a booth dashboard, upload cards through a 4-step AI-assisted flow, build drop announcements, and pay one flat monthly subscription with zero per-sale fees.
domain: trading card collectibles marketplace (sports, Pokemon, MTG)

explicit_requirements.text: Home/Map screen: a US map showing verified card shops as pins, color-coded by category.
Home screen shows drop announcement banners.
Home screen has search.
Home screen has category tiles.
Tapping a shop pin opens that shop's profile page.
Shop profile offers a value-box flip view: cards appear face-down and flip over one at a time with a fan animation, like digging through a bin at a real show.
Shop profile also offers a standard grid view of inventory.
Buyers can search inventory by player, team, or set.
Buyers can filter inventory by price and by category.
Buyers can save cards to a watchlist.
Buyers can message sellers directly (per-listing buyer/seller threads).
Buyers can make an offer on a listing (offer status tracked: pending/accepted/declined).
Buyers can buy instantly at the asking price.
Sellers apply and are manually verified before they can list anything (curation gate, not an open marketplace).
Approved sellers get a booth dashboard showing active listings, monthly sales total, open offers, and follower count.

Guided 4-step AI-assisted upload flow: (1) take a photo, (2) CollX API auto-detects player/set/year/grade, (3) seller reviews and sets a price with a market price suggestion shown, (4) publish as a standalone listing or add into a value box.
Drop announcement builder: seller sets title, date/time, and teaser description.
Drop announcement builder shows a live preview.
Drops are pushed to all of a seller's followers via in-app notification and email.
Flat monthly seller subscription billing via Stripe, recurring (not one-time), with zero fees on individual sales.
User role distinction between buyer and seller.
Buyers can follow a shop (follow relationship that feeds drop notifications).
Web plus mobile-responsive, with the ability to wrap for App Store/Google Play later via Median.co.

requested_stack.core: Bubble.io for the core app (web + mobile-responsive, wrappable for App Store/Google Play later via Median.co)
requested_stack.payments: Stripe recurring subscriptions (flat monthly seller fee, NOT one-time charges)
requested_stack.map: Google Maps or Mapbox plugin for the pin-based map
requested_stack.email: Klaviyo or similar for email notifications
requested_stack.ai: CollX API for card auto-detection (player/set/year/grade) in the upload flow

entities.name: Users
relationships: a buyer has Watchlist, Follows, Offers, Messages

entities.name: Shops
relationships: has many Listings, Value boxes, Drops, Followers

entities.name: Listings
relationships: optionally part of a Value box
has many Offers, Messages, Watchlist saves

entities.name: Value boxes
relationships: groups many Listings for the flip browsing experience

entities.name: Offers
relationships: links a Listing and a buyer (User)

entities.name: Messages
relationships: buyer-seller thread linked to a specific Listing

entities.name: Drops
relationships: fan-out to that shop's Followers for in-app + email notification

implicit_needs: Trust and curation are the product's spine: the manual verification gate must visibly signal a vetted, non-open marketplace.
Founding-seller onboarding flow: smooth seller application + approval, since the launch plan depends on signing 10 to 20 founding sellers (a fee-savings pitch doc already exists).
Phased delivery that ships a usable core (accounts, listings, map, Stripe) within budget, with the flip animation and AI upload as fast-follows.
Mobile-first responsive feel that emulates the tactile, browse-a-bin energy of an in-person card show.
Clear value-prop messaging around zero per-sale fees vs. competitor marketplaces (this is the headline differentiator).
A path to a native app later (Median.co wrap) without rebuilding.
engagement: Open to fixed price or hourly, prefers phased delivery: core first (accounts, listings, map, Stripe), with flip animation + AI upload as a fast-follow if needed to hit budget.
constraints: US-only freelancers (only freelancers located in the U.S. may apply)
success_signals: A live, clickable demo that makes the value-box flip experience feel real and delightful (the signature feature).
A US map with color-coded shop pins that opens a shop profile, proving the core browse loop.
Concrete acknowledgement of the zero-per-sale-fee subscription model and a credible Stripe recurring plan.
A clear phased delivery plan that hits the core within the 6 to 10 week window and the 2.5k to 4.5k budget.
Evidence we understand the trading-card domain and the in-person card-show vibe.
Comfort with the Bubble.io + CollX + Stripe + maps integration path (or a credible, justified equivalent).
red_flags: Budget (2.5k to 4.5k) is modest for the full scope described; phasing is essential and must be framed honestly.

Client requests Bubble.io specifically; our demo platform is static React (michaelwegter.com), so the demo must be framed as a high-fidelity interactive prototype of the experience, not the Bubble build itself.
CollX API access/terms and live card-grade detection are an integration dependency outside our control (assume mock detection in the demo).
US-only freelancer requirement is a hard gate to confirm we meet.

demo_concepts.name: The Value Box - flip-through-the-bin experience
demo_concepts.what: A shop profile where a value box of face-down cards fans out and flips over one at a time, with grid-view toggle, player/team/set search, price + category filters, and watchlist/offer/buy/message actions on each card.
demo_concepts.hero_feature: The tactile face-down-to-face-up card flip with a fan animation that recreates digging through a bin at a real card show.
demo_concepts.why_wins: It nails the client's single most distinctive, hardest-to-imagine feature in a way a Bubble screenshot never could, proving we can deliver the signature delight that sets Card Shopper apart.

demo_concepts.name: Card Shopper Map - find a shop, open the booth
demo_concepts.what: An interactive US map with color-coded category pins, drop-announcement banners, search and category tiles, where tapping a pin opens a shop profile that flows into the value-box/grid browse.
demo_concepts.hero_feature: The color-coded pin map that opens straight into a live shop profile, demonstrating the full buyer browse loop end to end.
demo_concepts.why_wins: It shows the entire buyer entry experience (the home screen the client described first) working as a cohesive flow, with mock shop/listing data, no backend required.

demo_concepts.name: Seller Booth - AI upload + drop builder + zero-fee dashboard
demo_concepts.what: A seller dashboard with stats (active listings, monthly sales, open offers, followers), the guided 4-step AI-assisted upload (photo to mock auto-detected player/set/year/grade to price-with-market-suggestion to publish/add-to-box), a drop announcement builder with live preview, and a Stripe-style flat-monthly subscription panel highlighting zero per-sale fees.
demo_concepts.hero_feature: The 4-step AI-assisted upload that auto-fills card details and suggests a market price, paired with the zero-fee subscription framing.
demo_concepts.why_wins: It speaks directly to the people the client must recruit first (founding sellers) and showcases the business-model differentiator; an optional Flask blueprint could stub a CollX-style detect endpoint, but mock data carries it fine.
demo_concepts.optional_backend: A small Flask blueprint could mock a CollX-style /detect endpoint returning player/set/year/grade + market price, if we want a live round-trip.
